orgDesign.companyProfile: Typical mid-size banking platform company with separate product lines for core banking, lending, embedded finance, and shared risk and platform operations. The organization is now modeled as a network of smaller teams sized around 10 FTE.
orgDesign.operatingModel: Banking product teams own product-line outcomes, while risk and platform teams provide reusable control and scale capabilities. Larger value streams are split into sibling teams so accountability stays close to concrete capability ownership.
designPrinciples: Product-line teams stay close to customer P&L and regulatory change.
Platform expansion capabilities are centralized to avoid duplicated control logic.
Partner onboarding and API experience are treated as a first-class value stream.
orgDesign.staffingPrinciple: Prefer teams of roughly 8-11 FTE, with broader customer streams split into multiple sibling teams inside the same group or team family.
orgDesign.groupingPrinciple: Use top-level groups for major value streams and a second layer of subgroups for team families when a stream contains multiple 10-FTE teams.
orgDesign.groupLeadershipPrinciple: When a group contains several 10-FTE teams, add lightweight group-level leadership and principal IC roles rather than scaling any single team too far beyond its local scope.
